preset_description: Dark = substance signal. Spotlights product capabilities and metrics. CTA and pricing are understated — features are the story, not conversion pressure.

brief.audience: Startups needing an AI-ready design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes context immediately: positions MODE as the AI-ready design system built for startups, signals relevance to the visitor's need, and frames the consideration journey ahead.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: With context set, articulate MODE's core capabilities — AI-readiness, component architecture, speed-to-ship — so the Validator archetype understands what they're actually evaluating before seeing proof.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators need trust signals before committing. Introduce customer logos, partner endorsements, and social proof from other startups to establish authority and reduce perceived risk at this consideration stage.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Critical for Validators: back up the value claims with concrete metrics, case study data, and measurable outcomes (e.g., time saved, adoption rates) that satisfy their need for proof before any purchase decision.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Now that the Validator has seen capabilities, credibility, and hard evidence, present pricing and plan comparison to frame the final choice clearly and move them from consideration toward commitment.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. Delivers a clear CTA to convert to paid, leveraging the accumulated proof and decision framing to prompt action with minimal friction.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat captures visitors not yet ready to convert with secondary paths — free trial, docs, newsletter signup — and provides standard footer navigation to prevent dead-end exits.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes context immediately: positions MODE as the AI-ready design system built for startups, signals relevance to the visitor's need, and frames the consideration journey ahead.

sections.name: Features
sections.rationale: With context set, articulate MODE's core capabilities — AI-readiness, component architecture, speed-to-ship — so the Validator archetype understands what they're actually evaluating before seeing proof.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators need trust signals before committing. Introduce customer logos, partner endorsements, and social proof from other startups to establish authority and reduce perceived risk at this consideration stage.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Critical for Validators: back up the value claims with concrete metrics, case study data, and measurable outcomes (e.g., time saved, adoption rates) that satisfy their need for proof before any purchase decision.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Consideration stage is pre-conversion; minimal variant recommended as it strips nav_links and keeps logo with single CTA only, preparing the visitor for decision without distraction.
Validator archetype always includes trust_signal, and with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster to establish credibility at the opening moment.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE resolves component behavior, visual emphasis, and copy register to the right experience for every user — without manual variants or QA overhead.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. No subscription. Lifetime access.

page.reasoning: Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions rather than feature names; 3-col-icon is the default structure for articulating product capabilities clearly.

slots.headline: What you're actually evaluating
slots.subhead: Six capabilities that separate MODE from a component library with good intentions.

features.title: Semantic resolution engine
features.description: Components resolve behavior across archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode — automatically. No conditional logic in your templates.

features.title: IA planner — brief to architecture in seconds
features.description: Powered by Claude Opus, the IA planner converts a single brief into a full page architecture. What used to take a whiteboard session takes a prompt.

features.title: Multi-variant generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. Ship three audience-calibrated experiences in the time it took to build one.

features.title: Palette map — visual emphasis on autopilot
features.description: Visual weight and accent resolve per component per funnel stage without manual assignment. The system accumulates emphasis toward conversion by default.

features.title: Component manifest — 11 fully specified components
features.description: Every component ships with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent. No guesswork about where a component belongs or what it should contain.

features.title: Full ownership — no lock-in, no runtime dependency
features.description: MODE lives in your codebase. Bring your own Anthropic API key, fork the kit freely, and extend without asking permission. You own the intelligence layer.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires role and company attribution for credibility; with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity, serving as the primary trust signal for this archetype.

slots.quote: We evaluated three design systems that claimed to be AI-ready. MODE was the only one where the intelligence was actually in the code — not a Figma plugin or a third-party dashboard. We were shipping intent-aware components inside the first sprint.

page.reasoning: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts as unsourced stats reduce trust; 3-stat is the preferred default and with-source establishes scale and credibility.

stats.label: Median time from install to first resolved component output
Page variants produced per brief versus manual workflow
Fully specified components with slot schemas, zone affinity, and usage intent — on day one

page.reasoning: Consideration stage benefits from recapturing intent; with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages to maintain momentum toward paid conversion.
